text: Equilibrium. An action profile ( a 1 , ..., a n ) is a Nash Equilibrium of the game Γ ( n ) if and only if

2.1 Threshold Games The game Γ ( n ) is a threshold game – agents have binary actions, and take one of the actions if the number of their friends taking that action exceeds some threshold. In our setting, the threshold: (1) is an absolute number of friends, and (2) is common to all agents. In models with this feature, the notion of a k -core is crucial to the analysis. The k -core of a network G is the largest sub-network H of G such that every agent in H has at least k friends in H [Seidman, 1983, Frieze and Karo ́nski, 2016]. 4 In fact, in our model, it is all that we need to characterise the equilibrium. Remark 1. The game Γ ( n ) has a unique Nash Equilibrium. Agents follow the old norm if and only if they are in the k -core of the network G .
